subtitle: MVP & Unified Platform Delivery
hero_sub: 15+ years leading complex technology initiatives from ambiguous business need to production outcome — including direct 0-to-1 and MVP delivery experience as a founder who built and shipped a platform end to end.

the_case.need: 7+ years TPM/Technical Program Management/Product Delivery/Systems Analysis
the_case.answer: 15+ years of technical delivery leadership across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, OVRSTCH, and more.
the_case.detail: Comfortably exceeds the stated threshold — technical product delivery has been the consistent throughline of my career.

the_case.need: MVP, prototype, product modernization, platform consolidation, or 0-to-1 delivery
the_case.answer: OVRSTCH: founded and delivered an MVP solo, concept to production. Understood.org: led a platform modernization/replatform initiative through UAT and launch. Stealth AI startup: 0-to-1 delivery from ambiguous research to production model.
the_case.detail: This is the strongest section of my background for this specific role — OVRSTCH is a literal 0-to-1 MVP I built and shipped myself, with no engineering team to hand it to. Understood.org is a direct platform modernization match. The stealth AI startup engagement was 0-to-1 delivery under real ambiguity.

the_case.need: Translate ambiguous business needs into structured technical requirements and delivery plans
the_case.answer: Core throughline across every engagement — from stealth AI startup research goals to Eptura's cross-functional Agile PMO transformation.
the_case.detail: Every consulting engagement started with ambiguity and required building the structure myself — requirements, backlogs, delivery plans — not inheriting it from someone else.

the_case.need: Cross-functional alignment across Product, Engineering, Finance, and business teams
the_case.answer: Eptura: Operations Board reporting across ~10 teams. Understood.org: primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering and executive stakeholders.
the_case.detail: Executive-level alignment across functions has been a constant, not an exception — I've been the named bridge between technical delivery and business decision-making at every recent engagement.

the_case.need: Leading technical integrations and managing dependencies across systems
the_case.answer: OVRSTCH: integrated payment (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay) and fulfillment (Printify API) systems solo. Eptura and Crocs: cross-team, cross-system dependency coordination at enterprise scale.
the_case.detail: The OVRSTCH integration work is direct, hands-on proof — I owned the technical integration decisions myself, with real cross-platform data dependencies and no team to delegate to.

the_case.need: .NET, SQL, and AWS experience (strongly preferred, not stated as a hard requirement)
the_case.answer: Cloud delivery background is Azure rather than AWS specifically; general SQL/database background (MySQL). .NET is not in my direct experience. Addressed honestly via the JD's own alternate path — prior developer/systems-focused experience.
the_case.detail: I'm not going to claim direct .NET or AWS experience I don't have. What I bring instead is exactly what this posting says can substitute: systems-focused technical fluency, built as a former front-end engineer and sharpened this year shipping 6 production applications hands-on via Claude Code.

the_stack.headline: The MVP delivery toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily or with direct delivery experience.

nodes.label: Jira & Azure DevOps
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency plus Azure/Kubernetes cloud delivery coordination
nodes.pm_role: Standardized at Eptura across ~10 teams. Coordinated release readiness on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org.

nodes.label: Payment & Fulfillment Integration
nodes.desc: Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay, Printify — hands-on API integration
nodes.pm_role: Personally architected and integrated every one of these systems at OVRSTCH as founder, with real cross-platform data dependencies.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Jira AI, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. 6 production apps shipped via Claude Code in 2026, demonstrating hands-on systems-level fluency.

nodes.label: SQL / Database
nodes.desc: General SQL and database background (MySQL, complex queries)
nodes.pm_role: Not .NET/AWS-specific, but a genuine, transferable database fluency confirmed through direct hands-on exposure on prior engagements.

nodes.label: Confluence, Smartsheet, GitHub Actions
nodes.pm_role: Used across every recent engagement for requirements documentation, RAID logs, and delivery automation.

when_things_break.scenario: An MVP needs to ship with real technical integration dependencies and no dedicated engineering team
response: At OVRSTCH, I was the only person — no team to hand any integration work to
Architected the commerce platform and integrated payment systems (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay)
Automated fulfillment via the Printify API with real cross-platform data dependencies
Diagnosed a production bottleneck and re-sequenced the workflow myself

when_things_break.scenario: A platform modernization initiative is distressed and needs to reach UAT and launch under real pressure
response: At Understood.org, inherited a distressed, multi-year platform modernization in its final crucial months
Led UAT planning and execution — test coordination, defect triage, sign-off
Championed accessibility compliance as a non-negotiable Definition of Done requirement
Result: delivered through QA/UAT with zero critical release regressions

when_things_break.scenario: Multiple business units and technical teams need to align on priorities before delivery can even start
response: At Eptura, ~10 distributed teams across Marketing, UX, Engineering, and Data had never worked from a shared cadence
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums as the primary cross-team alignment mechanism
Produced executive dashboards so leadership could see conflicts and dependencies before they became blockers
Result: 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover

how_i_run_a_room: Every initiative starts with structured discovery — bringing order to ambiguity before any requirement gets written down
Technical requirements, process flows, and decision records are documented as they're made, not reconstructed after the fact
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks and dependencies named before they become blockers
Executive status reporting anticipates questions before they're asked
UAT is planned as part of the delivery plan from day one, not bolted on before launch
Release readiness is a defined, documented gate — not a hope

points: Founded and delivered a 0-to-1 MVP solo, concept to production
Integrated payment (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay) and fulfillment (Printify API) systems
Owned technical integration planning with real cross-platform data dependencies
Diagnosed and resolved a production bottleneck, cutting cycle time ~25%

track_record.role: Technical Delivery Consultant / Platform Modernization Lead
points: Led a Sitecore-to-Contentful platform modernization to on-time launch
Managed a globally distributed team across 12+ countries
Led UAT planning and execution through sign-off
Primary technical point of contact for the VP of Engineering

track_record.role: Senior Technical Delivery Consultant / Agile Coach
points: Coordinated technical integration planning across ~10 distributed teams
Produced executive dashboards tracking delivery KPIs for senior leadership
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Acted as escalation point for delivery risk and cross-team blockers

track_record.role: Technical Delivery Consultant / Release Governance Lead
points: Owned release governance cadence and go/no-go decisions
Designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls in Jira workflow
Built 'Release Radar,' a real-time release-readiness tracking framework
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Copilot

before: MVP scope is unclear and business units disagree on priorities
Technical requirements get lost in translation between business and engineering
Cross-system dependencies surface late, threatening the delivery timeline
Executives find out about delivery risk after it's already a problem
after: A clear MVP scope with structured requirements everyone has aligned on
Technical requirements translated cleanly from business vision to engineering execution
Cross-system dependencies mapped and managed before they become blockers
Executive reporting that surfaces risk early enough to act on it

the_ramp.deliverable: Review current MVP scope, stakeholder map, and existing technical documentation; sit in on discovery sessions to understand where ambiguity remains.
Begin translating business requirements into technical specifications and delivery plans; identify the first cross-system dependency risk.
Own the MVP delivery roadmap and cross-functional alignment cadence; deliver the first executive status report; establish working relationships with Product, Engineering, and Finance stakeholders.
